title: The Three Years I Stopped Making Art

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I stopped making art for three years. <break time="350ms"/>Three years of nothing. <break time="300ms"/>I thought I'd never come back.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>I was in my studio one day. <break time="300ms"/>Blank canvas. <break time="250ms"/>Brush in hand. <break time="400ms"/>And I couldn't move. <break time="350ms"/>The voice was so loud: <break time="300ms"/>'You're not good enough. <break time="250ms"/>Everyone will see you're a fraud. <break time="200ms"/>Why bother?' <break time="500ms"/>I stood there for two hours. <break time="400ms"/>Then I walked out. <break time="350ms"/>I didn't go back for three years. <break time="500ms"/>I told people I was 'taking a break.' <break time="400ms"/>But I thought I was done forever. <break time="350ms"/>I thought imposter syndrome had won.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>It didn't win. <break time="400ms"/>I came back. <break time="350ms"/>Slowly. <break time="300ms"/>Quietly. <break time="500ms"/>Not because the voice went away - <break time="400ms"/>it didn't. <break time="350ms"/>But because I realized I didn't need to defeat it to create. <break time="300ms"/>I just needed to create alongside it. <break time="500ms"/>If you've stopped - <break time="400ms"/>if you think you're done - <break time="350ms"/>the door is never closed. <break time="300ms"/>It's just waiting for you to open it again.

when_to_share: When someone expresses wanting to give up on their creative work entirely
